channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Mary Meeker says AI and higher education need to team up
item.description: Influential tech investor Mary Meeker published a new report Monday calling for the tech industry, government, and higher education to all work together to advance artificial intelligence during what she sees as an inflection point “of epic proportions.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: The Supreme Court just ducked a major decision in its social media ‘censorship’ cases
item.description: In a unanimous opinion Monday, the Supreme Court declined to rule on the constitutionality of laws in Florida and Texas that would require online platforms to publish political speech they would otherwise remove.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: The Supreme Court will decide if Florida and Texas are violating social media First Amendment rights
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court is expected on Monday to rule on the legality of Republican-backed laws in Florida and Texas intended to restrain social media companies from curbing content the platforms deem objectionable — statutes the industry has argued violate the free speech rights of these businesses.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Sorry, Morgan Freeman is not narrating that viral TikTok video
item.description: Actor Morgan Freeman calls out people using AI to make fake versions of his voice for their own purposes.
item.dc:creator: erichs211@gmail.com (Eric Hal Schwartz) , TechRadar

item.title: The technology powering Taylor Swift, Netflix and the Sphere
item.description: Disguise is a pioneer in an exploding market for visual graphics in demand from Hollywood to the NBA.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Huawei claims its AI chip is faster than Nvidia A100 — but Chinese powerhouse struggles to match transformational pace of rivals as international sanctions force change in design
item.description: Huawei had to get creative with the design of its Ascend 910B AI chip in order to compete with Nvidia A100.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams) , TechRadar

item.title: Nutanix hunts disgruntled VMware customers
item.description: The new Nutanix is virtually unrecognizable from the company that created the hyperconverged infrastructure (HCI) market in 2011.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps on hold efforts in Texas and Florida to regulate social media platforms
item.description: The Florida and Texas laws were signed by Republican governors in the months following decisions by Facebook and Twitter, now X, to cut then-President Donald Trump off over his posts related to the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol by his supporters.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court punts on Texas, Florida social media laws
item.description: The need for Supreme Court review grew more dire when federal appeals courts reached opposite conclusions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: LockBit ransomware attack stole data on millions of Infosys McCamish users
item.description: At first, IMS thought 57,000 people were affected by the cyberattack - but the reality is much worse.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DoD’s Joint Staff GenAI sprint lays out 8 internal use cases
item.description: Lt. Gen. Todd Isaacson, the CIO for the Joint Chiefs of Staff/J-6, said a review team is completing recommendations for leadership around using GenAI. The post DoD’s Joint Staff GenAI sprint lays out 8 internal use cases first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Prevent Scam Calls: Techniques for Tracing and Blocking Anonymous Phone Numbers
item.description: Learn how to identify and block hidden or anonymous phone numbers with expert CyberGuy Kurt Knutsson, and stay protected from unwanted calls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alibaba to cease data center operations in India and Australia
item.description: Alibaba’s cloud computing unit, otherwise known as Alibaba Cloud or Aliyun, has decided to cease data center operations in India and Australia.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: European Union accuses Facebook owner Meta of breaking digital rules with paid ad-free option
item.description: European Union regulators have accused social media company Meta Platforms of breaching the bloc’s new digital competition rulebook by forcing Facebook and Instagram users to choose between seeing ads or paying to avoid them.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Autonomous trash-gobbling robot boat wages war on waterway waste
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson provides the details on the Class 3 Clearbot: an autonomous vessel for efficient marine trash/plant removal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 AI-powered apps that’ll actually save you time
item.description: You can’t open your eyes these days without seeing something about generative AI and all the reasons it’s, like, totally gonna revolutionize the way you work. And yet, call me surly, but most of the AI tools out there at this point seem far less impressive in practice than they do on paper.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11 Insider Previews: What’s in the latest build?
item.description: The Windows 11 2023 Update has been released, but behind the scenes, Microsoft is constantly working to improve the newest version of Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 7 steps to improve analytics for data-driven organizations
item.description: When leaders say they want to be a data-driven organization, a key objective is empowering business people to use data, predictive models, generative AI capabilities, and data visualizations to improve decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: InfoWorld Announces Technology of the Year Awards 2024 Nominations
item.description: The InfoWorld Technology of the Year Awards 2024 recognize innovative products across software development, cloud computing, data analytics, and AI & machine learning categories; nominations now open for best technology solutions
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Artificial Intelligence Portrayals of Instagram Creators Imminent
item.description: Meta's Upcoming 'AI Beta' Tag to Identify AI-Generated Content from Influencers, Potentially Revolutionizing Digital Presence
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: FCC proposes rule requiring carriers to unlock all cellphones
item.description: The FCC wants carriers to unlock cellphones within 60 days of purchase so users can shop different networks.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, The Hill

item.title: Manage Your Passwords: Effective Solutions for Password Overload
item.description: With hundreds of passwords to remember, it's no wonder people struggle to keep track. Discover practical strategies to simplify your password management
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Opinion: Big tech companies are burning through reservoirs of trust with AI
item.description: Is generative AI a useful productivity tool or a series of overhyped, intrusive products that consume vital resources?
item.dc:creator: Irina Raicu, The Mercury News

item.title: The Supreme Court nears the end of another momentous term. A decision on Trump's immunity looms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the last 10 days of June, on a frenetic pace of its own making, the Supreme Court touched a wide swath of American society in a torrent of decisions on abortion, guns, the environment, health, the opioid crisis, securities fraud and homelessness.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As AI gains a workplace foothold, states are trying to make sure workers don't get left behind
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — With many jobs expected to eventually rely on generative artificial intelligence, states are trying to help workers beef up their tech skills before they become outdated and get outfoxed by machines that are becoming increasingly smarter.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon is reviewing whether Perplexity AI improperly scraped online content
item.description: Amazon is reviewing claims that the artificial intelligence startup Perplexity AI is scraping content — including from prominent news sites — without approval.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration
item.description: How will healthcare continue to transform? The post Navigating the future of healthcare: Transformation, measurement and integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: White House to hold conference for digital creators
item.description: The Biden administration will host digital creators at the White House for the first-ever Creator Economy Conference in August, the director of digital strategy announced Friday. The conference, which will be held Aug.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google opens access to 2 million context window of Gemini 1.5 Pro
item.description: Google has opened access to the 2-million-token context window of the Gemini 1.5 Pro AI model. The company also is giving developers access to code execution capabilities in the Gemini API and making the Gemma 2 model available in Google AI Studio. These announcements were made on June 27.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: EU commissioner slams Apple Intelligence delay
item.description: If you believe European Commission Vice President Margrethe Vestager, Apple’s decision to delay introduction of its Apple Intelligence AI is a “stunning declaration” of its own anticompetitive behavior.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI success: Real or hallucination?
item.description: Ever consider how much of AI is a hallucination? Not how much AI output is hallucination, but rather how much of what we hear or read about it is genuine? Could it be that AI success is mostly hype, and that all the hype may be hurting the technology rather than helping? Enterprises need to get to t
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Supreme Court Rules in Favor of Biden Administration in Social Media Dispute with Conservative States
item.description: The US Supreme Court has ruled in favor of the Biden administration, allowing federal efforts to combat controversial social media posts on topics like COVID-19 and election security. The decision was made in a dispute with Republican-led states over the scope of government power.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Where tech, politics & giving meet: CEO Nicole Taylor considers Silicon Valley's busy intersection
item.description: Nicole Taylor has an insider's view of philanthropic trends from her seat as the president and CEO of the Silicon Valley Community Foundation.
item.dc:creator: THALIA BEATY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: AI image-generator startup Stability AI looks for reshoot with new CEO and investor group
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The troubled artificial intelligence company behind image-generator Stable Diffusion is looking for a reshoot with a new CEO and a surge of investment from a group that includes Sean Parker, Facebook’s former president and Napster's co-founder.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia won't pay an $8 million ransom after a cyberattack compromised the national data center
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesia's national data center has been compromised by a hacking group asking for a $8 million ransom that the government says it won't pay.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Car dealerships in North America revert to pens and paper after cyberattacks on software provider
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Car dealerships in North America are still wrestling with major disruptions that started last week with cyberattacks on a company whose software is used widely in the auto retail sales sector. CDK Global, a company that provides software for thousands of auto dealers in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Music record labels sue AI song-generators Suno and Udio for copyright infringement
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Big record companies are suing artificial intelligence song-generators Suno and Udio for copyright infringement, alleging that the AI music startups are exploiting the recorded works of artists from Chuck Berry to Mariah Carey.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
